question.text: In the context of the PFUTP Regulations, what constitutes 'illegal mobilization of funds'?

answer.text: Collecting deposits from the public by a non-banking financial company
Sponsoring, causing to be sponsored, carrying on or causing to be carried on any collective investment scheme by any person
Raising capital through an IPO without SEBI approval
Raising funds through a rights issue without board approval

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 4(2)(p) includes illegal mobilization of funds by sponsoring or causing to be sponsored or carrying on or causing to be carried on any collective investment scheme by any person. **The Trap:** Option A is governed by SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, not PFUTP. Option C is governed by RBI regulations for NBFCs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Collective Investment Schemes are covered in NISM Series III-A and V-A exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Unauthorized CIS schemes are a major area of SEBI enforcement - always verify if a scheme is registered before recommending it to clients.
